channel.title: Policy & Security Think Tank Updates
channel.description: Custom RSS feed from CSIS, CFR, and other policy institutions

item.title: Thomas Christensen in Foreign Affairs: Will China Overplay Its Hand?
item.description: China enters talks with Washington feeling confident—but that confidence may be misplaced. If Beijing overestimates its leverage against the United States, fragile progress could unravel and tensions could rise again.

item.title: Alberta Separatism Would Harm Both Canada and the United States
item.description: CSIS Americas Program Deputy Director and Senior Fellow Christopher Hernandez-Roy and Randy Boissonnault explore the Alberta separatist movement and how it has impacted U.S.-Canada relations.

item.title: Chicken Soup Coming Home to Roost
item.description: This week, Bill Reinsch analyzes the impact of the Trump administration’s steel tariffs, highlighting how they have raised the cost of goods and added complexity to compliance efforts.
